reason: The WRF em_quarter_ss ideal build was deferred within the F7H time-box. The Gen2 canonical tree (~/src/canairy_meteo/Gen2/artifacts/wrf_gpu_src/WRF) is built ONLY for em_real (main/wrf.exe, real.exe present; no ideal.exe). Building ideal.exe for em_quarter_ss requires `./compile em_quarter_ss`, which rewrites object files in the canonical tree and would change the pinned Gen2 baseline sha (project rule: must not modify in place). A clean separate-tree copy + configure + compile is a multi-GB, long build outside the empirical Phase-1 time-box.
fallback: Analytic discrete-balance + WRF-source line audit (done) localized the discrepancy without the binary:

wrf_source_findings.pg_buoy_w_inputs: module_em.F:1362 CALL pg_buoy_w(rw_tend, p, cqw, mu, mub, ...) -- the `p` actual argument is grid%p = the FULL-perturbation calc_p_rho_phi diagnostic (NOT a small-step work-delta), and `mu` = grid%mu_2 (full perturbation dry mass).
wrf_source_findings.grid_p_source: WRF builds grid%p via calc_p_rho_phi (module_big_step_utilities_em.F:1029 al, :1083-1087 p) from the FULL ph', mu', theta'; rk_step_prep (module_em.F:49-227) does NOT recompute grid%p, so pg_buoy_w consumes the grid%p left by the PREVIOUS RK step's closing calc_p_rho_phi (solve_em.F:6180,:7542).
wrf_source_findings.jax_bug_localized: The JAX operational stage fed pg_buoy_w `calc_p_rho_wrf(prep).p` (the small-step WORK-DELTA pressure built from prep.ph_work~=0 and prep.mu_work~=0), and carried state.p_perturbation = the calc_p_rho_step work pressure (O(1-10 Pa)) instead of the WRF calc_p_rho_phi diagnostic (O(1e3-1e4 Pa)). Both have been corrected (see ph_coupling_fix.md).

definitive_arbiter_remaining: A future WRF em_quarter_ss ideal build (seeding M9 per-operator parity) is still the definitive AC4 arbiter for the RESIDUAL discrepancy (the vertical 2dz acoustic mode that still detonates the bubble ~180s). The analytic audit fixed the buoyancy-source/pressure-restoring inconsistency but cannot by itself rule out a coefficient-level (c2a/epssm/calc_coef_w) mismatch in the implicit-w tridiagonal solve; that needs the binary.
